info.title: WAX API - Custom Products
info.description:
Endpoints for the custom product negotiation flow. A custom product originates from a 3D model generated by Meshy AI (via n8n). Once submitted, it goes through an automated quotation and a price negotiation cycle between the admin and the customer before being automatically added to the cart.

## Complete lifecycle

```
[n8n webhook] → POST /api/CustomProduct  (customer submits)
                    ↓
              [System auto-quotes price]
                    ↓
              Status: AwaitingAdminReview
                    ↓
  POST /api/CustomProduct/{id}/admin/proposals  (admin proposes price)
                    ↓
              Status: AwaitingCustomerReview
                    ↓
  POST /api/CustomProduct/{id}/proposals  (customer counter-offers, optional)
  OR
  POST /api/CustomProduct/{id}/approve    (customer accepts)
                    ↓
              Status: Approved  →  [auto add to cart]
                    ↓
              Status: AddedToBasket  →  [normal checkout flow]
```

## State machine

| Status | Description | Who can act |
|---|---|---|
| `PendingQuotation` | System is calculating the price | System (automatic) |
| `AwaitingAdminReview` | Waiting for admin to propose a price | Admin |
| `AwaitingCustomerReview` | Waiting for customer to accept or counter-offer | Customer |
| `Approved` | Both parties agreed on a price | — |
| `AddedToBasket` | Product was automatically added to the customer's cart | — |
| `Rejected` | Product was rejected by admin or customer | — |

## Negotiation rules

- After system quotation, admin **must propose** a price (cannot directly approve the system quote).
- After admin proposes, customer can **accept** (approve) or **counter-offer** (propose).
- After customer counter-offers, admin can **accept** or propose again.
- Approval by the customer always triggers automatic addition to cart.
- Approval by the admin of a customer counter-offer also triggers automatic addition to cart (uses the basket cookie the customer sent in their last interaction).
- Either party can trigger rejection at any point before approval.

tags.name: CustomProduct - Customer
tags.description: Endpoints available to any authenticated user: submit, view, propose price, approve

tags.name: CustomProduct - Admin
tags.description: Endpoints restricted to users with the `Admin` role: list all, propose price, reject

post.description:
Registers a new custom product coming from the Meshy AI / n8n flow. The backend immediately runs an automated quotation based on the design parameters and transitions the product to `AwaitingAdminReview`.

**Important:** Send the basket cookie along with this request (`Cookie: .AspNetCore.Identity.Application=...`). The server reads the basket cookie automatically to link the customer's current basket to the product — this basket will be used later if the admin approves the customer's counter-offer.

Returns the full `CustomProductDto` including the system-generated quotation as the first proposal.

example.rawDescription: A custom engagement ring with a blue gemstone

200.description: Custom product submitted and quoted successfully. Status is `AwaitingAdminReview`.

example.name: Ring
example.description: A custom engagement ring with a blue gemstone

400.description: Validation error or a custom product with the same `taskId` already exists

example.error: Already exists a custom product with that TaskId

get.description: Returns all custom products belonging to the authenticated user, in any status. Useful for the customer's dashboard to track the negotiation state of each piece.

200.description: List of custom products for the authenticated user

proposals.comment: We can offer this piece at a lower price given the material.

get.description: Returns the full details of a specific custom product including all price proposals. Customers can only access their own products. Admins can access any product.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the custom product

example.name: Ring
example.description: A custom engagement ring with a blue gemstone

proposals.comment: We can offer this piece at a lower price given the material.

403.description: Customer trying to access another user's product

get.description: Returns all custom products across all users. Supports optional filtering by status. Intended for the admin panel to manage the negotiation queue.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Optional status filter. When omitted, returns products in all statuses.

200.description: List of custom products matching the filter

403.description: User does not have the `Admin` role

post.description:
Admin submits a price proposal for a custom product. The product must be in `AwaitingAdminReview` status.

After this call the status transitions to `AwaitingCustomerReview` — the customer will see the proposal and can accept or counter-offer.

The admin is also required to propose a price even if they agree with the system's automated quote. This is intentional: it forces an explicit review and ensures the admin is aware of the final number before the customer sees it.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the custom product

example.comment: We can offer this piece at 6000 given the resin material and complexity.

200.description: Price proposal registered. Status is now `AwaitingCustomerReview`.

proposals.comment: We can offer this piece at 6000 given the resin material and complexity.

example.error: The admin can not propose in this state

403.description: User does not have the `Admin` role

post.description:
Customer submits a counter-offer price. The product must be in `AwaitingCustomerReview` status and the authenticated user must be the product owner.

After this call the status transitions back to `AwaitingAdminReview` — the admin will see the customer's counter and can accept or propose again.

**Important:** Send the basket cookie with this request. The server captures the basket cookie to keep track of which cart should receive the product if admin later accepts this counter-offer.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the custom product

example.comment: Would it be possible at 5200? That's my budget limit.

proposals.comment: We can offer this piece at 6000.
Would it be possible at 5200? That's my budget limit.

400.description: Product is not in `AwaitingCustomerReview` state, or the user is not the product owner

example.error: The client can not propose in this state

post.description:
Accepts the latest price proposal. Behavior depends on the caller's role:

**Customer approves** (status must be `AwaitingCustomerReview`):
- Validates that the basket cookie is present and the basket exists.
- Marks the last proposal as accepted and sets `agreedPrice`.
- Transitions status to `Approved`.
- Automatically triggers addition to the cart via an integration event — **no further action needed from the frontend**. Poll `GET /api/Basket` to confirm the item appears.

**Admin approves a customer counter-offer** (status must be `AwaitingAdminReview` and last proposal source is `Customer`):
- Marks the last proposal as accepted and sets `agreedPrice`.
- Transitions status to `Approved`.
- Automatically triggers addition to the customer's cart using the basket they sent in their last interaction (submit or counter-offer). The frontend should notify the customer that the product was approved and added to their cart.

**Preconditions for customer approval:**
- The basket cookie must be present (the server reads it automatically from the request).
- The basket identified by that cookie must exist in the system.

**Preconditions for admin approval of customer counter-offer:**
- The customer must have submitted or counter-offered at least once (so a `basketId` is on record). If they haven't, the server returns 400.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the custom product

200.description: Price approved. Status is `Approved`. The product will be added to the cart asynchronously — poll `GET /api/Basket` after a short delay (1–2 seconds).

proposals.comment: We can offer this piece at 6000.

400.description: Cannot approve in current state, basket missing, or customer basket not on record for admin approval

value.error: can not approve in this state

unknownBasket.summary: Admin approving counter-offer but customer never sent a basket

value.error: Customer basket is unknown; cannot complete approval

403.description: Customer trying to approve another user's product

404.description: Custom product not found, or basket not found for customer approval

post.description:
Admin permanently rejects a custom product. The product transitions to `Rejected` status and cannot be approved or further negotiated.

Rejection is only allowed before the product reaches `Approved` or `AddedToBasket` status.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Unique identifier of the custom product

schema.description: Reason for rejection — shown to the customer
schema.example: The design complexity exceeds our current production capacity.

application/json.example: The design complexity exceeds our current production capacity.

200.description: Custom product rejected successfully. Status is now `Rejected`.

400.description: Product is already `Approved` or `AddedToBasket` and cannot be rejected

example.error: can not reject a product approved

403.description: User does not have the `Admin` role

SubmitCustomProductRequest.description: Payload sent by the frontend after receiving the Meshy AI model from the n8n webhook

taskId.description: Meshy AI task identifier — serves as a unique reference for the 3D model. Duplicate submissions with the same `taskId` are rejected.

glbUrl.description: Public URL to the `.glb` 3D model file generated by Meshy AI

rawDescription.description: Original text description the customer provided to Meshy AI to generate the model
rawDescription.example: A custom engagement ring with a blue gemstone

CustomProductDesignRequest.description: Design parameters extracted from the Meshy AI generation result. These are used by the automated quotation engine to estimate the price.

type.description: Category or type of the piece (e.g. Ring, Necklace, Bracelet, Pendant). Also used as the product name if no explicit name is provided.

material.description: Primary material of the piece — impacts the quotation calculation

dimensions.description: Dimensions of the piece in millimeters as `widthxheightxdepth`. Used by the quotation engine to calculate volume and complexity.

details.description: Optional additional design details or finishing notes

ProposeCustomPriceDto.description: Price proposal payload, used by both admin and customer

amount.description: Proposed price in the system's base unit (cents). For example, 6000 represents $60.00.

comment.description: Optional message to accompany the proposal. Visible to the other party.
comment.example: We can offer this piece at a lower price given the material.

CustomProductDto.description: Full representation of a custom product, including its current status, design, price history, and negotiation proposals

id.description: Unique identifier of the custom product

name.description: Display name of the piece — defaults to the `design.type` value if not explicitly set

description.description: Original description the customer provided to Meshy AI
description.example: A custom engagement ring with a blue gemstone

price.description: Current working price in base units (cents). Reflects the last proposal amount. Once approved, matches `agreedPrice`.

taskId.description: Meshy AI task identifier used to reference the 3D model

glbUrl.description: URL to the `.glb` 3D model. Use this to render the model in the frontend (e.g. with `<model-viewer>` or Three.js). Note: this is a `.glb` file, not an image.

ownerUserId.description: User ID of the customer who owns this custom product

status.description: Current status in the negotiation lifecycle

agreedPrice.description: Final agreed price in base units. `null` until both parties approve. Populated when status reaches `Approved` or `AddedToBasket`.

proposals.description: Chronological list of all price proposals. The last entry is always the active proposal. Use this array to render the negotiation history.

createdAt.description: Date and time when the product was submitted (UTC)

updatedAt.description: Date and time of the last status or price update (UTC). `null` if never updated after submission.

PriceProposalResponse.description: A single price proposal entry in the negotiation history

comment.description: Optional message attached to this proposal by the proposer
comment.example: We can offer this piece at a lower price given the material.

isAccepted.description: Whether this proposal was accepted. Only one proposal will have `isAccepted: true` — the final agreed one.

createdAt.description: Date and time when this proposal was registered (UTC)

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error response returned by the API on 4xx responses

cookieAuth.name: .AspNetCore.Identity.Application
cookieAuth.description: ASP.NET Identity authentication cookie. Obtained via POST /api/login?useCookies=true.
